items.title: Kernel trick replaces dot products
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  x["input x"] --> dot["need x dot x prime"]
  dot --> swap["replace with K of x x prime"]
  swap --> phi["implicit phi space"]
  phi --> linear["linear algorithm e.g. SVM"]
items.caption: Algorithms that only need inner products can use K instead of building φ(x) explicitly.

items.title: RBF similarity vs distance
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  near["points close in input space"] --> high["K near 1"]
  far["points far apart"] --> low["K near 0"]
  gamma["gamma controls falloff rate"]
items.caption: RBF kernel K(x,x′)=exp(−γ‖x−x′‖²) maps small distances to high similarity; large γ makes similarity spike only locally.

items.title: Linear boundary in phi-space
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  input["original x not separable"] --> map["implicit phi via K"]
  map --> hyperplane["hyperplane in phi-space"]
  hyperplane --> curve["curved boundary in original space"]
items.caption: A linear separator in the lifted φ-space appears as a nonlinear decision boundary in the original inputs.
